witnesses.description: One topological order for a tiny prerequisite DAG.
A second valid topological order swaps the independent algebra and analysis vertices.

checks.claim: The listed prerequisite order is a valid topological order.
checks.notes: The validator checks that every vertex appears once and that every directed edge points from an earlier vertex to a later vertex.

checks.claim: The algebra and analysis vertices can be swapped while preserving the topological order.
checks.notes: The validator accepts the swapped order because there is no edge between algebra and analysis.

checks.claim: The order intro, topology, algebra, analysis, thesis is a valid topological order.
checks.notes: The listed order puts topology before algebra even though algebra must precede topology.

checks.claim: The order places algebra at position 2 and topology at position 1 while the edge algebra -> topology requires algebra before topology.

data.certificate: produce_evidence emits checked QF_LIA arithmetic evidence for the exact integer contradiction 2 < 1

checks.notes: The SMT-LIB artifact isolates the edge-position contradiction after replay identifies the violating edge.

checks.claim: The directed cycle a -> b -> c -> a has a topological order.
checks.notes: A total order of the cycle vertices must place one cycle edge backward; the listed cycle is replayed as the finite obstruction.

checks.claim: General DAG topological-sort correctness and linear-extension existence require future theorem-prover reconstruction.

data.target_theorem_shape: Every finite acyclic directed graph has a topological order, and any directed cycle obstructs such an order.
data.future_checker: Lean proof of finite DAG linear-extension existence, Kahn/DFS topological-sort invariants, and cycle-obstruction completeness.

checks.notes: The checked rows cover two explicit orders and one explicit cycle obstruction. They do not prove algorithm correctness or asymptotic runtime.
